factual_core: Twentieth-century poet useful for discussing precision, observation, restraint, travel, and descriptive pressure without quoting poems.
short_intro: A poetry anchor for careful observation, formal restraint, and descriptive intelligence.
entry_points: compare with Robert Lowell through voice, restraint, and personal material

conversation_moves.overview: Position Bishop as a poet of precise observation and restraint.
conversation_moves.works_list: Do not invent a full bibliography; give bounded entry points if works are not carded.
conversation_moves.representative_works: Use approved work cards only; otherwise stay at method level.
conversation_moves.entry_path: Start with short poems and compare voice with Lowell, without quoting.
conversation_moves.explain_work: Explain form and observation without reproducing poetry.
conversation_moves.compare: Compare Bishop and Lowell by voice, personal material, observation, and restraint.
conversation_moves.why_it_matters: Explain how restraint can carry pressure without confession.
conversation_moves.quote_or_lyrics_boundary: No full poems or long excerpts; summarize themes and form.

copyright_policy: Use metadata, themes, and short commentary only; no poems or long quoted text.
source_summary: Public general cultural knowledge abstracted into structured metadata, not source prose.
boundary_notes: Not active as public semantic content.

factual_core: Bishop and Lowell can be compared by restraint versus confession, observation versus pressure, and different uses of personal material.
short_intro: A relation card for comparing two poetry voices without reducing either to biography.
entry_points: compare voice first, then personal material and form

conversation_moves.overview: State that this is a comparison relation, not a biography shortcut.
conversation_moves.works_list: Do not list uncaptured poems as if fully covered.
conversation_moves.entry_path: Start from voice and form differences.
conversation_moves.explain_work: Not applicable unless a work card exists.
conversation_moves.compare: Mention both Bishop and Lowell and at least one comparison axis.
conversation_moves.why_it_matters: The pair tests how personal material can be handled with different formal temperatures.
conversation_moves.quote_or_lyrics_boundary: No full poems or long excerpts.

safe_boundaries: do not turn style into diagnosis
copyright_policy: No poem excerpts; summarize formal and thematic differences.
source_summary: Public general literary knowledge abstracted into a relation card.
boundary_notes: Not active as public semantic content.

factual_core: Poetry and fiction can be interpreted through different units: poems often foreground line, image, rhythm, and compression; novels foreground scene, plot, narration, time, and character systems.
short_intro: A method relation for distinguishing genre-level interpretation without asking a vague counterquestion.
entry_points: compare units of reading: line/image/rhythm versus scene/narration/time

conversation_moves.overview: Explain the interpretive units of each genre.
conversation_moves.entry_path: Start with what the form makes visible.
conversation_moves.explain_work: Apply to a named poem or novel if supplied.
conversation_moves.compare: Mention poetry and fiction plus comparison axes.
conversation_moves.why_it_matters: Prevents turning all literature into one mood vocabulary.

safe_boundaries: do not reduce literature to emotion
ask for a specific work only after answering the general distinction
copyright_policy: No long excerpts; use form-level explanation.
source_summary: General literary method card, not source prose.
boundary_notes: Not active as public semantic content.

names: 不要把所有文学都讲成情绪
factual_core: Literary interpretation should use form, genre, period, narration, language, and evidence; emotion can be one effect, not the whole answer.
short_intro: A method card that blocks mood-only literary answers.
entry_points: separate emotion, form, genre, period, and evidence

conversation_moves.overview: Say why emotion is insufficient and name the missing dimensions.
conversation_moves.works_list: If asked for a list, give works or authors, not mood labels.
conversation_moves.representative_works: If asked for works, list concrete works only when carded.
conversation_moves.entry_path: Use form, genre, period, and evidence as the entry path.
conversation_moves.explain_work: Apply to the work without flattening it to a feeling.

copyright_policy: No long excerpts; use abstract method language.

provenance.label: Britannica:不要把所有文学都讲成情绪

boundary_notes: Not active as public semantic content.
